titleEn: Second 10-hectare expansion for Swedish grower officially opening during the Frövi Festival
titleZh: 瑞典番茄种植商第二座10公顷温室在弗勒维节期间正式启用
summaryZh: 瑞典ECA Operator Frövi AB公司第二座10公顷番茄温室将在2026年8月22日弗勒维节期间正式启用。该温室利用附近造纸厂的余热供暖，减少对天然气的依赖，旨在加强全年本土番茄生产。总经理强调人员管理对温室运营成功至关重要，并计划通过数据驱动的气候、灌溉和营养系统实现可持续生产。
translationZh:
随着ECA Operator Frövi AB公司准备在瑞典弗勒维种植其第二座10公顷番茄温室，重点不仅在于提高产能，还在于应用运营第一座设施所积累的经验。此次扩建是在第二座温室完工后进行的，这是该公司利用可控环境农业和工业余热加强全年本土番茄生产目标的一部分。

ECA Operator Frövi AB公司总经理Dumitru Mititelu表示，扩建的决定是基于从第一个生产周期中获得的信心。“第一座温室教会了我们很多，不仅关于番茄种植，还关于建立强大的组织、团队合作以及更好地理解市场。”

他补充说，第二座温室不仅仅是为了生产更多番茄。“而是关于把我们学到的一切做得更好。”

**人员仍是温室性能的核心**
尽管现代温室技术发挥着重要作用，但Dumitru认为运营成功最终取决于管理作物的人员。“真正让温室成功的是人。”

他解释说，第一座温室的经验强化了早期让运营人员参与、建立清晰工作流程以及投资培训的重要性。“每一茬作物都会教你新东西，植物是最大的老板。你必须保持谦逊，每天不断改进。”

他将温室运营比作赛车运动。“你可以建造世界上最现代化的温室，但如果没有积极进取、技术娴熟的人员，它永远无法发挥全部潜力。这就像买了一辆一级方程式赛车，却让一个不懂赛车的人来驾驶。”

他表示，公司一直专注于打造一个员工相互学习、承担责任并持续改进的工作场所。

**从第一天起建立作物**
随着种植期的临近，优先事项将是在生产的前几周建立平衡的生长环境。“我们将专注于通过保持气候、灌溉和营养之间的适当平衡，让植物有一个健康的开端。”每日作物监测将使团队能够快速响应植物需求的变化。“良好的开端对整个季节的剩余时间产生巨大影响。”

**能源整合支持可持续生产**
新温室将继续利用现代气候控制、灌溉和生产系统来支持数据驱动的决策。然而，他表示该项目的一个显著特点仍然是其能源模式。“与欧洲许多温室运营依赖天然气不同，我们使用附近造纸厂的余热。”

温室利用附近造纸厂的余热供暖，而不是主要依赖天然气。据该公司称，这种方法利用了原本会被浪费的能源，同时减少了对化石燃料的依赖。

对于Dumitru来说，创新不仅仅局限于温室设备。“创新不仅仅是技术或人工照明。它还在于找到更聪明的合作方式、高效利用资源以及持续改进我们所做的事情。”

第二座温室的正式启用定于2026年8月22日弗勒维节期间举行，届时行业合作伙伴和利益相关者将有机会参观新设施的运营情况。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了工业余热与温室种植结合的可持续能源模式，对智慧农业中能源管理创新有参考价值，但内容偏重传统种植管理，与智慧畜牧核心（IoT/AI/自动化）关联度中等。

 “the greenhouse will use surplus heat from the nearby paper mill”

 Second 10-hectare expansion for Swedish grower officially opening during the Frövi Festival

 

 
 As ECA Operator Frövi AB prepares to plant its second 10-hectare tomato greenhouse in Frövi, Sweden, the focus is not only on increasing production capacity but also on applying the experience gained from operating its first facility. The expansion follows the completion of the second greenhouse, which forms part of the company's aim to strengthen year-round domestic tomato production using CEA and industrial surplus heat.

 Dumitru Mititelu, General Manager at ECA Operator Frövi AB, says the decision to expand was based on the confidence gained from the first production cycle. "The first greenhouse has taught us a lot, not only about growing tomatoes, but also about building a strong organisation, working together as a team and understanding the market better."

 He adds that the second greenhouse isn't just about producing more tomatoes. " It's about taking everything we've learned and doing it even better."

 © ECA Operator Frovi

 People remain central to greenhouse performanceWhile modern greenhouse technology plays an important role, Dumitru believes operational success ultimately depends on the people managing the crop. "It's really people that make a greenhouse successful."

 He explains that experience from the first greenhouse reinforced the importance of involving operational staff early, establishing clear work routines and investing in training. "Every crop teaches you something new, and the plants are the biggest boss. You have to stay humble and keep improving every day."

 He compares greenhouse operations to motorsport. "You can build the most modern greenhouse in the world, but without motivated and skilled people, it will never reach its full potential. It's like buying a Formula 1 car and putting someone behind the wheel who doesn't know how to race."

 He says the company has focused on developing a workplace where employees learn from one another, take responsibility and contribute to continuous improvement.

 © ECA Operator Frovi

 Establishing the crop from day oneWith planting approaching, the priority will be to establish a balanced growing environment during the first weeks of production.

 © ECA Operator Frovi"We'll focus on getting the plants off to a healthy start by keeping the right balance between climate, irrigation and nutrition." Daily crop monitoring will allow the team to respond quickly to changing plant requirements. "A good start makes a huge difference for the rest of the season."

 Energy integration supports sustainable productionThe new greenhouse will continue to utilise modern climate control, irrigation and production systems to support data-driven decision-making. However, he says one of the project's defining features remains its energy model.

 "Instead of relying mainly on natural gas, like many greenhouse operations across Europe, we use surplus heat from the nearby paper mill."

 The greenhouse uses surplus heat from a nearby paper mill rather than relying primarily on natural gas for heating. According to the company, this approach makes use of energy that would otherwise go unused while reducing reliance on fossil fuels.

 For Dumitru, innovation extends beyond greenhouse equipment. "Innovation isn't only about technology or artificial lighting. It's also about finding smarter ways of working together, using resources efficiently and continuously improving what we do."

 The official opening of the second greenhouse is scheduled to take place during the Frövi Festival on 22 August 2026, where industry partners and stakeholders will have the opportunity to see the new facility in operation.

 For more information:ECA Operator Frovi ABDumitru Mititelu, General Manager+46 (0)738987325[email protected]
